originalTitle: Sudan accuses Ethiopia and UAE of orchestrating drone attacks on airport
title: Friends, Airports, and Flying Things!
content:
Hello from Your African News Friend!

Hi everyone! Your friendly news reporter from Africa here, ready to share some important news in a super simple way! Today, we're talking about countries, friends, and how we can all live happily together. Africa is a really big continent, full of amazing people, animals, and places. We have many, many countries, just like you have many friends in your school or neighbourhood. It's like a big, big family!

Our Big African Family

Imagine three houses in a neighbourhood. One house is called Sudan, another is Ethiopia, and a little further away, there's a house called the United Arab Emirates, or UAE for short. These are three countries, and they are like big neighbours. Sometimes, even good neighbours or friends have a little trouble or disagreement. It's like when you and your friend both want to play with the same toy. It can make everyone a little sad or confused. Did you know? Africa has 54 countries! That's a lot of neighbours and friends!

What Happened in Sudan?

Well, some grown-ups in Sudan were feeling a bit sad. They have special grown-ups called 'ministers' who speak for their whole country. A minister is like the head of a family, who tries to make sure everyone is safe and happy. This minister from Sudan said that he thinks some grown-ups from Ethiopia and the UAE did something tricky at an airport in Sudan. He said it was like they chose the 'wrong path' and might be sad about it later.

What is an Airport Anyway?

An airport is a very special place! It's like a big parking lot, but for airplanes! Airplanes are those amazing big birds that fly high in the sky and take people to different places. At an airport, planes land when they arrive and take off when they leave. People go to airports to visit family far away, or to go on holidays, or even to send important packages to other countries. It's very important for airports to be safe and quiet, so everyone can travel without worries. Imagine if your playground wasn't safe – that wouldn't be fun, right? An airport needs to be safe for everyone. Did you know? The biggest airport in Africa is in Johannesburg, South Africa!

Flying Things: Friend or Trouble?

The minister from Sudan talked about 'drone attacks'. Now, 'drones' are like small flying machines. Some are tiny, like toys that can fly around your garden and take pictures. Others are bigger, and grown-ups use them for different jobs. Sometimes, drones can be very helpful! They can deliver things, or help look for lost animals, or even take beautiful pictures from high up in the sky. They are like robots that fly! But sometimes, if people use them in the wrong way, they can cause trouble. It's like if someone plays with a toy too roughly and breaks something by mistake. The grown-ups in Sudan were worried that some flying things caused trouble at their airport. We all want flying things to be used for good, helpful reasons, and not to make anyone worried or sad. We want happy skies! Did you know? Some drones are so small they can fit in the palm of your hand!

Friends Talk Things Out

When you and your friend have a disagreement, what do you do? Do you shout? Or do you talk about it calmly? Usually, talking calmly helps you understand each other and become friends again. It's the same for countries! When countries have a problem, the best way to solve it is to talk. They can have meetings, just like you have show-and-tell, but they talk about important grown-up things. The minister from Sudan probably hopes that everyone can sit down and talk about what happened. He wants everyone to understand each other and find a way to be friends again, like good neighbours should be. Talking helps make sure that everyone can live in peace and harmony, which means everyone is happy and gets along. Did you know? In Africa, people speak many different languages! But they still find ways to talk and understand each other.

Wishing for Peace

What does 'peace' mean for you? It means you can play with your friends, go to school, read your favourite books, and feel safe and happy at home. It means no worries or big fights. When countries are friends and talk to each other, it helps everyone have peace. Children can go to school, families can travel to visit each other, and everyone can work and play without feeling scared. So, when the minister from Sudan said he hopes everyone takes the 'right path', he means he hopes everyone will choose to be kind, talk, and be friends. That's the best path for everyone! We all wish for peace, not just in Sudan, Ethiopia, and the UAE, but all over Africa and the whole wide world! We want happy smiles and friendly waves.

Let's All Be Friends!

So, what's the most important thing we can learn today? It's that even if grown-ups and countries have disagreements, the best way forward is always to talk, to listen, and to try to be friends. Just like in your classroom or on the playground, being kind and understanding makes everything better. Let's send a big wish for friendship and peace to Sudan, Ethiopia, and the UAE! Thank you for listening, and remember to always be kind to your neighbours, big or small! See you next time, from your friendly reporter in Africa!
summary: Some grown-ups in Sudan felt sad because they think other grown-ups from Ethiopia and UAE caused some trouble at an airport with flying things, and we hope everyone can talk and be friends again.
whyMatters: It's important because when countries are friends, everyone is safe and happy, and we can all play and learn together without worries, which is the best way to live.
